commands.review_summary:
Adds Azure snapshot/restored-disk SKU options, parallel snapshot-fork provisioning and cleanup, force-delete/absence verification for Azure teardown, plus Azure docs and Go coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/performance PR rather than a bug report. The PR body provides live Azure validation for the new SKU path, fork readiness, cleanup timing, and resource absence checks.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is identified; maintainers need to resolve the stacked dependency and accept the Azure lifecycle and credential-boundary behavior before merge.

target.title: perf(azure): accelerate snapshot lifecycle
target.body:
## Why

Azure Windows snapshot forks remove machine bootstrap from the critical path, but the remaining Azure resource lifecycle still dominates short desktop-test runs. Snapshot and restored-disk storage also need an explicit performance choice instead of inheriting Azure defaults that may optimize for cost rather than fork latency.

This PR keeps Crabbox's existing defaults unchanged while adding an opt-in low-latency path and shortening the independent Azure create/delete work around each snapshot-backed lease.

## What Changed

- Add `azure.snapshotSKU` / `--azure-snapshot-sku` for checkpoint storage and `azure.osDiskSKU` / `--azure-os-disk-sku` for restored managed disks.
- Validate snapshot SKUs against Azure's supported snapshot values and restored-disk SKUs against the Azure SDK's managed-disk values.
- Create snapshot-fork networking and the restored OS disk concurrently, canceling sibling work on failure and preserving all errors.
- Set VM attachment delete options so snapshot-fork NICs and OS disks are owned by the VM lifecycle.
- Request force deletion for the VM, delete its remaining NIC, public IP, and OS disk concurrently, and verify all four resources are absent before reporting success.
- Document the opt-in `Premium_LRS` checkpoint and restored-disk configuration.

## How to Test

1. Build Crabbox from this branch and acquire a bootstrapped Azure Windows source lease using [#698](https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/698).
2. Create a checkpoint with `--azure-snapshot-sku Premium_LRS` and confirm the Azure snapshot uses `Premium_LRS`.
3. Fork it with `--azure-os-disk-sku Premium_LRS` and confirm the restored OS disk uses `Premium_LRS`, the lease reaches ready state, and the desktop remains reachable through the SSH-forwarded VNC endpoint.
4. Stop the fork and confirm the VM, NIC, public IP, and restored OS disk are all absent.
5. As a regression check, omit both flags and confirm the existing Azure defaults still apply; pass an unsupported snapshot SKU and confirm Crabbox rejects it before provisioning.

## Live Azure Proof

- Created Premium checkpoint `chk_6afb38a80907fbe4` with Azure snapshot `codex-asqa-premium-v2` (`Premium_LRS`, 30 GiB).
- Forked canary lease `cbx_fd697b9f9d65` as `Standard_D4s_v5` with a `Premium_LRS` restored OS disk; the lease reached ready state in 183.84s.
- Deleted the canary in 29.59s and verified that no VM, NIC, public IP, restored disk, or Crabbox lease remained.
- Used the resulting checkpoint for Sqatch retained-target validation, including a full passing Codex App smoke run.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/providers/azure ./internal/cli`
- `go vet ./...`
- focused `go test -race` coverage for the changed Azure provider and CLI paths
- full `go test ./...`; the full repository race run only reports the pre-existing macOS temporary-directory cleanup race, while the changed packages pass in isolation with `-race`

## Stack

1. [#698](https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/698) Azure Windows snapshot forks
2. #731 Azure snapshot lifecycle performance (this PR)

## Dependency

This PR is stacked on #698 at `72d5e2bfd06585b495febddbab306548473d406d`. Since the base branch belongs to the contributor fork, GitHub cannot represent that branch as the base of an upstream PR; the current `main` comparison therefore includes #698 until it merges. Rebase this branch onto updated `main` after #698 lands to reduce the review diff to commits `9d032e8b` and `4f891e43`.
